challenges.name: No Eating After 8pm
challenges.description: Late-night snacking flagged six times in fourteen days of journal entries — the kitchen closes before the willpower does.
challenges.protocol: Kitchen closed after 8pm; herbal tea allowed.
challenges.hoped_outcome: Fewer late-night snacking flags in the journal by the end of the week — honestly small, one week is one data point.

challenges.name: Morning Zone-2 Block
challenges.description: The movement pillar sits in Foundation tier while the calendar keeps eating the afternoon slot.
challenges.protocol: 30 minutes of zone-2 cardio before 9am, five days a week.
challenges.hoped_outcome: A visible cluster of morning sessions in the training log — enough to test whether the AM slot survives contact with real weeks.

challenges.name: Screens Off By Ten
challenges.description: Sleep-onset latency drifted up alongside late screen time in the evening journal notes.
challenges.source_detail: confirmed hypothesis: late screens → sleep onset +18min (2 checks)
challenges.protocol: All screens off by 10pm; paper book allowed.

challenges.name: Cold Shower Finish
challenges.one_liner: End every shower with 60 seconds cold — a daily rep of voluntary discomfort.
challenges.evidence_summary: Small trials suggest mood and alertness effects; the discipline rep is the real product.

challenges.name: Protein-First Breakfast
challenges.one_liner: 30g of protein before anything else in the morning.
challenges.evidence_summary: Higher-protein breakfasts reliably improve satiety in controlled trials.

challenges.name: Phone-Free First Hour
challenges.one_liner: No phone for the first hour after waking.
challenges.evidence_summary: Attention-fragmentation research supports protecting the first hour.

challenges.name: Evening Walk After Dinner
challenges.one_liner: A 15-minute walk within 30 minutes of finishing dinner.
challenges.evidence_summary: Post-meal walking blunts the glucose excursion in repeated crossover trials.
